converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Sarah E. Thomas papers
Thomas, Sarah (Sarah E.)
13-1-3599
23.1 cubic feet. 23.1 cubic feet.
1996-2006. Correspondence, Printed Materials, Research Materials Alphabetical subject files documenting activities as Carl A. Kroch Cornell University Librarian relating to all aspects of administration. Collection material in English 6081788
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Rachel Brill
Date completed:
August 2007
EAD encoding:
Evan Fay Earle, August 2007
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, May 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Sarah Thomas came to Cornell in 1996 from the Library of Congress, where she held positions overseeing the library's public services, special collections and cataloging directorate. She was the Carl A. Kroch Cornell University Librarian and Under Thomas' leadership, the library continued to build its rare and special collections and embraced the digital age, developing a suite of online services and offering 24-hour worldwide access to a rich array of online journals and other electronic media. Thomas was the principal force behind Project Euclid, a Mellon Foundation-funded initiative to support the cost-effective online dissemination of mathematics and statistics journals. While at Cornell she served on the executive board of the Association of Research Libraries for six years and was its president in 2004-05. She also chaired the Digital Library Federation Steering Committee and was a member of the board of the Research Libraries Group.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Alphabetical subject files documenting activities as Carl A. Kroch Cornell University Librarian relating to all aspects of administration. Includes files relating to various digital initiatives, the opening of the Libe Cafe, the addition to Mann Library and renovations to many of the existing libraries and the building of a off-site high-density warehouse. Also, files documenting her professional involvement in organizations such as the executive board of the Association of Research Libraries, the Digital Library Federation Steering Committee and as a member of the board of the Research Libraries Group.
Cite As:
Sarah Thomas papers, #13-1-3599 Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access restricted to the permission of the office of origin.
Academic libraries -- New York (State) -- Ithaca -- Administration. Library architecture -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Library buildings -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Library administrators -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Research Libraries Group Project Euclid Digital Library Federation,. Steering Committee Association of Research Libraries
